info.name: Lifey API
info.description:
REST API collection for the Lifey fitness & nutrition tracker.

Base path: `/api/v1`. Set the `baseUrl` collection variable (default `http://localhost:8080`).

**Implementation status:** All endpoints implemented: *Auth*, *Nutrition - Foods*, *Nutrition - Recipes*, *Nutrition - Meals*, *Exercises*, *Workouts*, *Weight Tracking*, *Statistics*, *Settings*, *Water Tracking*, *Steps*, *User Details*, *User Avatar*, *Trainer Invites*, *Client Invites*, *My Trainers*, *Trainer Clients*, *Trainer Client Data*, *Trainer Schedules*, *Trainer Assignments*, *Super Admin*.

All `/api/v1/**` routes require a JWT access token except `/api/v1/auth/register`, `/api/v1/auth/login`, `/api/v1/auth/social/google`, `/api/v1/auth/refresh`, `/api/v1/auth/forgot-password`, `/api/v1/auth/reset-password`, and the public `/api/v1/trainer-invites/email/**` invite-response link — note `/api/v1/auth/logout` requires auth too, it is NOT public. `/api/v1/trainer/**` additionally requires `ROLE_TRAINER` and `/api/v1/superadmin/**` requires `ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN`. The collection is set up to send the bearer token automatically: run *Auth > Register* once and then *Auth > Login* — the login request's test script stores the returned tokens in the `accessToken`/`refreshToken` collection variables, which the collection-level Bearer auth then attaches to every other request. When the access token expires (15 min), run *Auth > Refresh* to rotate both tokens.

Foods and exercises are per-user (see V40__foods_exercises_ownership.sql): the first real user in the database keeps the original seeded exercise rows (ids 1-8: Bench Press, Squat, Deadlift, Overhead Press, Barbell Row, Pull Up, Bicep Curl, Plank), every other user gets their own copy with different ids. Both are managed via their CRUD endpoints; deletes are soft (tombstoned) and no longer return 409 when still referenced.

Many list endpoints additionally support a mobile offline delta-sync feed via `?updatedSince=<ISO-8601 instant>` (see docs/16-delta-sync-rollout.md) and/or pagination via `?page=`; see the 'Delta sync ...' / '... (paged...)' requests in each folder.

Statistics use rolling periods ending now: daily = today, weekly = last 7 days, monthly = last 30 days. `latestWeight` is the most recent weight entry overall; `totalWater` sums water entries over the same period.

Settings is a per-user singleton: there's no create/delete, just *Get* (creates the row with defaults on first access) and *Update* (full replace). `unitSystem`, `theme` and `language` are all required.

Water Tracking has two parts: *Water Sources* (reusable presets like "Creatine Shake" = 0.9L, full CRUD) and *Water Entries* (logged intake, list/create/delete only — like Weight Tracking). An entry's `volumeLiters` is always client-supplied (picking a source just pre-fills it); `sourceId` is optional and purely informational.

The *Trainer ...* / *Super Admin* folders are the personal-trainer module (docs/personal_trainer/): a `ROLE_TRAINER` account manages invites/clients/schedules/assignments under `/api/v1/trainer/**`, clients respond to invites and view their trainers under `/api/v1/trainer-invites/**` and `/api/v1/my-trainers`, and a `ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN` account grants/revokes `ROLE_TRAINER` under `/api/v1/superadmin/**`.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Implemented feature. Registration, login, JWT access/refresh-token issuance, refresh-token rotation, and logout (single device / all devices).

request.description: Creates an account. `email` must be unique (case-insensitive); `password` must be 8-100 characters; `firstName`/`lastName` are required. Every new account gets `ROLE_USER`. Returns 201 with the created user, or 409 if the email is already registered. Optionally send the `X-Utc-Offset-Minutes` header so day boundaries follow the caller's timezone.
Demonstrates the 409 response when the email is already registered. Run 'Register' first.

Validates credentials and issues a token pair: a 15-minute JWT access token and a 30-day opaque refresh token (stored hashed server-side). Stores both in the `accessToken`/`refreshToken` collection variables, which every other request in this collection sends as `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}`. Returns 401 on a wrong email/password (same message either way, so login can't be used to enumerate accounts).
Demonstrates the 401 response for a wrong password.

Rotates the refresh token: the given token is revoked and a brand-new access/refresh pair is issued and stored. Returns 401 if the refresh token is unknown, expired, or already revoked (reuse of an already-rotated token is treated as a possible leak).

Revokes the given refresh token (logs out this device only). Requires a valid access token (this endpoint is NOT in the public-endpoints list, unlike register/login/refresh). Returns 204 regardless of whether the token was already revoked/unknown.
Revokes every active refresh token belonging to the authenticated user (logs out all devices). Requires a valid access token. Returns 204.
Verifies a Google-issued ID token, then finds, links, or creates the matching Lifey user and returns the same token pair as password login. Public endpoint.
Sends a 6-digit reset code by email. Always returns 200 regardless of whether the email is registered, so this endpoint can't be used to enumerate accounts. Public endpoint.
Resets the password using the 6-digit code emailed by 'Forgot password'. `code` must match `\d{6}`; `newPassword` must be 8-100 characters. Public endpoint.

Changes the current (authenticated) user's password. Revokes every refresh token for the user (including the one used for this request) and returns a fresh token pair, so the calling device stays logged in.

item.name: Weight Tracking
item.description: Implemented feature. Daily body-weight entries.

request.description: Returns all weight entries ordered by date descending. Optionally bounded to a date range via `from`/`to` (either or both may be omitted).

query.description: ISO-8601 instant — required, switches to the delta-sync feed

request.description: Backs the mobile offline sync pull. `updatedSince` (ISO-8601 instant) is required; ordering is fixed to updatedAt,id ascending, and a non-null `deletedAt` on a returned row is a tombstone. Response is a standard Spring Data page (accepts normal `page`/`size` params too).
Creates a weight entry. `date` must be today or in the past; `weight` must be positive. Returns 201 and stores the new id in the `weightId` variable.
Demonstrates the 400 validation response (future date + non-positive weight).
Deletes the weight entry with the captured id. Returns 204, or 404 if not found.

item.name: Nutrition - Foods
item.description: Implemented feature. Manually-created foods with per-100g macros.

request.description: Unpaged: always returns the full catalog. Any request carrying a `page` param is routed to the paged/search/delta-sync handler instead (see 'List foods (paged/search/delta sync)').

query.description: ISO-8601 instant — switches to the delta-sync feed (ignores `search`, includes hidden/deleted rows)

request.description: Backs the web foods table and the mobile offline sync pull. `search` case-insensitively matches on name. `updatedSince` switches to the delta-sync feed: `search` is ignored, hidden/deleted rows are included, ordering is fixed to updatedAt,id ascending, and a non-null `deletedAt` on a returned row is a tombstone. Response is a standard Spring Data page.
Returns a single food by id, or 404 if not found.

Creates a food. `name` is required and must be unique (case-insensitive); `caloriesPer100g` and `proteinPer100g` are required and must be >= 0; `carbsPer100g`/`fatPer100g`/`barcode`/`hidden` are optional (`hidden` defaults to false). `barcode`, when set, must be unique across the catalog. Returns 201, or 409 if the name (or barcode) is already taken. Stores the new id in the `foodId` variable.

Looks up a food by barcode. Returns the existing catalog entry if one is already tagged with this barcode (`source: LOCAL`), otherwise queries OpenFoodFacts (`source: OPENFOODFACTS`, `id` is null since it isn't persisted yet — POST /foods to save it). Returns 404 if neither has usable nutrition data for this barcode.
Demonstrates the 400 validation response (blank name, negative calories, missing protein).
Demonstrates the 409 response when a food with this name (case-insensitive) already exists. Run 'Create food' first so the name is taken.
Updates the food with the captured id. Returns 200, or 404 if not found.
Deletes the food with the captured id. Returns 204, or 404 if not found.

item.name: Nutrition - Recipes
item.description: Implemented feature. Recipes with a nested ingredient list referencing foods by id. The example bodies use the `{{foodId}}` captured by the Foods > Create food request, so run that first.

request.description: Returns all recipes with their ingredients.
Backs the web recipes view. `search` case-insensitively matches on name; omit it to page through everything. Response is a standard Spring Data page.

query.description: ISO-8601 instant — required, switches to the delta-sync feed

request.description: Backs the mobile offline sync pull. `updatedSince` is required; ordering is fixed to updatedAt,id ascending, and a non-null `deletedAt` is a tombstone. Ingredients are not independently delta-synced — whenever a recipe appears here, replace all of its local ingredients.
Returns a single recipe by id, or 404 if not found.

Creates a recipe. `name` required, at least one ingredient required, each ingredient needs an existing `foodId` and positive `quantityInGrams`. `description` (max 2000 chars), `favorite` and `servings` (positive) are optional — `servings` defaults to 1 if omitted (kept nullable for backward compatibility with older clients). Returns 201 and stores the new id in `recipeId`. Unknown foodId returns 404.
Demonstrates the 400 validation response (blank name, empty ingredients).
Replaces the recipe's fields and ingredient list. Returns 200, or 404 if recipe/food not found.
Deletes the recipe (and its ingredients) by id. Returns 204, or 404 if not found.

item.name: Nutrition - Meals
item.description: Implemented feature. Logged meals with a nested entry list referencing foods by id and a meal type (BREAKFAST/LUNCH/DINNER/SNACK). The example bodies use the `{{foodId}}` captured by Foods > Create food, so run that first.

request.description: Returns all meals (newest first) with their entries.

query.description: ISO-8601 instant — required, switches to the delta-sync feed

request.description: Backs the mobile offline sync pull. `updatedSince` is required; ordering is fixed to updatedAt,id ascending, and a non-null `deletedAt` is a tombstone. Entries are not independently delta-synced — whenever a meal appears here, replace all of its local entries.

Logs a meal. `dateTime` and `mealType` required, at least one entry required, each entry needs an existing `foodId` and positive `quantityInGrams`. `name` (max 255 chars) is optional. Returns 201 and stores the id in `mealId`. Unknown foodId returns 404.

Demonstrates the 400 validation response (missing mealType, empty entries). Note: unlike Weights/Steps/Water, `dateTime` has no past/future restriction on meals — a future dateTime alone would NOT be rejected.
Replaces the meal's fields and entry list. Returns 200, or 404 if meal/food not found.
Deletes the meal (and its entries) by id. Returns 204, or 404 if not found.

item.name: Exercises
item.description: Implemented feature. CRUD for the current user's own exercise list referenced by workout templates and sessions (exercises are per-user, like foods — see V40__foods_exercises_ownership.sql). The very first real user in the database keeps the original seeded rows (ids 1-8: Bench Press, Squat, Deadlift, Overhead Press, Barbell Row, Pull Up, Bicep Curl, Plank); every other user gets their own copy with different ids. Deletes are soft (tombstoned via `deletedAt`, surfaced through delta sync) — deleting an exercise still referenced by a template/session no longer returns 409.

request.description: Returns all exercises ordered by name.

query.description: ISO-8601 instant — required, switches to the delta-sync feed

request.description: Backs the mobile offline sync pull. `updatedSince` is required; ordering is fixed to updatedAt,id ascending, and a non-null `deletedAt` is a tombstone (exercises are soft-deleted).
Returns a single exercise by id, or 404.

Creates an exercise. `name` required; `category` (MuscleGroup: CHEST/BACK/SHOULDERS/BICEPS/TRICEPS/FOREARMS/QUADS/HAMSTRINGS/GLUTES/CALVES/ABS/CARDIO/FULL_BODY/OTHER), `equipment` (BARBELL/DUMBBELL/MACHINE/CABLE/BODYWEIGHT/SMITH_MACHINE/OTHER) and `description` are optional. Returns 201 and stores the id in `exerciseId`.
Renames the exercise. Returns 200, or 404 if not found.
Soft-deletes the exercise (tombstoned, not removed). Returns 204, or 404 if not found.

item.name: Workouts
item.description:
Implemented feature. Workout templates (named lists of exercises, each with an optional target set count) and logged workout sessions. Both reference exercises by id (manage them via the Exercises folder; each user has their own exercise list).

A workout template's body is `exercises`: a list of `{ exerciseId, targetSets }` (not a flat `exerciseIds` array).

A session has two independent lists: `exerciseIds` (planned exercises — e.g. copied client-side from a template's `exercises[].exerciseId` when starting from one) and `sets` (sets actually logged, with reps/weight/`performedAt`). Both can be empty: a session can be started blank with neither, and exercises/sets are filled in as the workout progresses. Planned exercises are a snapshot only — there's no link back to the template via `exerciseIds`, though the session can optionally record `templateId` for reference. Each set now requires a `performedAt` timestamp; `reps`/`weight` are intentionally unvalidated (an incomplete set is a normal transient client-side state, not a bad request) and rows missing both are silently dropped server-side.

request.description: Returns all workout templates with their exercises.

query.description: ISO-8601 instant — required, switches to the delta-sync feed

request.description: Backs the mobile offline sync pull. `updatedSince` is required; ordering is fixed to updatedAt,id ascending, and a non-null `deletedAt` is a tombstone. Exercise links are not independently delta-synced — whenever a template appears here, replace all of its local exercise links.

Creates a template. `name` required, at least one entry in `exercises` required. Each entry needs an existing `exerciseId` (else 404); `targetSets` is optional and must be positive if present. Returns 201 and stores the id in `templateId`.
Demonstrates the 400 validation response (blank name, empty exercises).
Returns a single template by id, or 404 if not found.
Renames the template and replaces its exercise list. Returns 200, or 404 if the template/exercise is not found.
Deletes the template by id. Returns 204, or 404 if not found.
Returns all sessions (newest first) with their planned exercises and logged sets.
An additive alternative to the unpaged list, for callers that want to page through a long history (newest first) instead of pulling everything at once.

query.description: ISO-8601 instant — required, switches to the delta-sync feed

request.description: Backs the mobile offline sync pull. `updatedSince` is required; ordering is fixed to updatedAt,id ascending, and a non-null `deletedAt` is a tombstone. Sets and planned exercises are not independently delta-synced — whenever a session appears here, replace all of its local children.

Logs a session. `startedAt` required (today or past), `finishedAt` optional. `exerciseIds` and `sets` are both required fields but may be empty arrays. Each entry needs an existing exerciseId (else 404). Each set needs a required `performedAt` (today or past); `reps`/`weight` are intentionally unvalidated — an incomplete set is a normal transient state, not a bad request, and is silently dropped server-side if both are missing. `activeCalories`, `averageHeartRate`, `healthWorkoutId` (Apple Health import) and `templateId` (only read on create) are all optional. Returns 201 and stores the id in `sessionId`.

Demonstrates starting a session from a template: the client resolves the template's `exercises[].exerciseId` (Workouts > Get workout template) and sends them here as the session's planned `exerciseIds`, with no sets logged yet; `templateId` is recorded for reference (read only on create). The response's `exercises` array lists them; `sets` fills in as the workout progresses via Update workout session.
Demonstrates starting a session with nothing planned (no template, no sets yet). Returns 201; both `exercises` and `sets` are empty in the response.

Replaces the session's start/finish time, planned-exercise list, and set list (e.g. finish an in-progress workout, or log sets for a session that was started empty/from a template). Returns 200, or 404 if the session/exercise is not found.
Deletes the session (and its sets) by id. Returns 204, or 404 if not found.

item.name: Statistics
item.description: Implemented feature. Aggregated totals over rolling periods: total calories & protein from logged meals, workout session count, and the latest weight entry.

query.description: Caller's local date (yyyy-MM-dd); defaults to the server's current date
Caller's local date (yyyy-MM-dd); defaults to the server's current date
Caller's local date (yyyy-MM-dd); defaults to the server's current date

item.name: Settings
item.description: Implemented feature. Per-user preferences: unit system, optional daily calorie/macro/water/step goals, theme, language. One row per user, created with defaults on first access — there's no separate create endpoint.

request.description: Returns the current user's settings, creating the row with defaults (`unitSystem: METRIC`, `theme: SYSTEM`, `language: SYSTEM`, no goals set) if this is the first access.

Replaces the current user's settings. `unitSystem` (METRIC/IMPERIAL), `theme` (LIGHT/DARK/SYSTEM) and `language` (SYSTEM/ENGLISH/HUNGARIAN) are required; the daily goal fields (`dailyCalorieGoal`/`dailyProteinGoal`/`dailyCarbsGoal`/`dailyFatGoal`/`dailyWaterGoalLiters`/`dailyStepGoal`) are optional (omit/null for no goal) and must be >= 0 (`dailyStepGoal` must be strictly positive) if present. Returns 200.
Demonstrates the 400 validation response (missing unitSystem/theme/language, negative calorie goal).

item.name: Water Tracking
item.description: Implemented feature. Reusable water-intake presets (*Water Sources*) and logged intake (*Water Entries*).

request.description: Returns all of the current user's water sources.
Creates a water source. `name` required; `volumeLiters` required and must be > 0. Returns 201 and stores the new id in `waterSourceId`.
Demonstrates the 400 validation response (blank name, non-positive volume).
Returns a single water source by id, or 404 if not found.
Renames the source and/or changes its preset volume. Returns 200, or 404 if not found. Past entries logged from this source keep their original (snapshotted) volume.
Deletes the water source. Returns 204, or 404 if not found. Past entries logged from it are kept, with their `sourceId` cleared.
Returns all of the current user's logged water entries (newest first).

query.description: ISO-8601 instant — required, switches to the delta-sync feed

request.description: Backs the mobile offline sync pull. `updatedSince` is required; ordering is fixed to updatedAt,id ascending, and a non-null `deletedAt` is a tombstone.
Logs a manual entry (no source). `consumedAt` required (today or past); `volumeLiters` required and must be > 0. Returns 201 and stores the id in `waterEntryId`.

item.name: Log water entry - from a source (example)

request.description: Demonstrates logging from a water source: the client reads the source's `volumeLiters` (List water sources) and sends it here as a pre-filled value the user could still adjust. `sourceId` must reference an existing source (else 404) but is purely informational — the server always uses the `volumeLiters` sent, never re-derives it from the source.
Demonstrates the 400 validation response (future consumedAt, non-positive volume).
Deletes the water entry. Returns 204, or 404 if not found.

item.name: Steps
item.description: Implemented feature. Daily step counts — one entry per date per user (upserts on create). Full CRUD via list/create/update/delete.

request.description: Returns all of the current user's daily step counts (newest first). Optionally bounded to a date range via `from`/`to` (either or both may be omitted).

query.description: ISO-8601 instant — required, switches to the delta-sync feed

request.description: Backs the mobile offline sync pull. `updatedSince` is required; ordering is fixed to updatedAt,id ascending, and a non-null `deletedAt` is a tombstone.
Records a step count (upserts on date). `date` is required (no past/future restriction); `steps` must be >= 0. Returns 201 and stores the new id in `stepId`.
Demonstrates the 400 validation response (negative steps). Note: the future date does NOT itself cause a 400 — `date` has no past/future restriction.
Updates an existing step count entry. Returns 200, or 404 if not found.
Deletes the step count entry. Returns 204, or 404 if not found.

item.name: User Details
item.description: Implemented feature. Onboarding biometrics (gender, birth date, height, activity level, primary goal, optional target weight) and the suggested daily calorie/macro/water goals derived from them.

request.description: Returns the current user's onboarding details. Returns 404 if the user hasn't completed onboarding yet.

Creates or replaces the current user's onboarding details. `gender` (MALE/FEMALE/UNSPECIFIED), `birthDate`, `heightCm` (80-250), `activityLevel` (SEDENTARY/LIGHT/MODERATE/ACTIVE/VERY_ACTIVE) and `primaryGoal` (LOSE_WEIGHT/MAINTAIN/GAIN_MUSCLE) are required; `targetWeightKg` (30-300) is optional. Used both for the initial onboarding submit and later edits from Settings. Returns 200.

Persists only the fields named in `fields` (GENDER/BIRTH_DATE/HEIGHT_CM/ACTIVITY_LEVEL/PRIMARY_GOAL/TARGET_WEIGHT_KG) — the client always sends its full, already-valid current form state, and everything not listed is ignored/kept as-is. Recalculates and applies the daily calorie/macro/water goals to Settings. Used by the Settings edit confirmation popup. Returns 200.

Stateless — computes suggested daily calorie/macro/water goals from the request body (mirrors 'Upsert user details' plus current `weightKg`); nothing is persisted, so the onboarding wizard can preview goals before the user commits to anything. Returns `bmr`/`tdee`/`calories`/`proteinGrams`/`carbsGrams`/`fatGrams`/`waterLiters`.

item.name: User Avatar
item.description: Implemented feature. Profile picture upload/download for the current user.

request.description: Returns the current user's profile picture as binary image data. Supports conditional GET via If-None-Match/ETag (304 Not Modified). Returns 404 if no picture is set.

Uploads or replaces the current user's profile picture as multipart/form-data with a `file` part. Accepts JPEG/PNG up to 5MB; the server re-encodes it to a center-cropped 512x512 JPEG, stripping metadata. Returns 204.
Removes the current user's profile picture. Returns 204.

item.name: Trainer Invites
item.description: Implemented feature (personal-trainer module). Trainer-side (web admin) invite management — requires ROLE_TRAINER. Client-side counterpart is *Client Invites*; public email-link counterpart is *Trainer Invites (email)*.

request.description: Invites a client by exact email. `email` required. Requires ROLE_TRAINER. Returns 201 and stores the id in `inviteId`.
Lists this trainer's pending (non-expired) invites.
Cancels a pending invite. Returns 204.

item.name: Trainer Invites (email)
item.description: Implemented feature (personal-trainer module). Public, unauthenticated accept/decline link sent in the trainer invite email — protected by an opaque, single-use, per-invite token instead of a session. Gated by `lifey.trainer-invite.email-enabled`; the in-app accept flow (*Client Invites*) is unaffected.

request.description: Accepts or declines a pending invite via its emailed token. `accept=false` declines. Returns an HTML confirmation page (200) even for an expired/already-used token.

item.name: Client Invites
item.description: Implemented feature (personal-trainer module). Client-side (mobile) view of trainer invites — plain authenticated ROLE_USER, no special role required.

request.description: Lists this user's pending (non-expired) trainer invites. Polled on app start/foreground.
Accepts or declines a pending invite. `accept` required. Returns 204.

item.name: My Trainers
item.description: Implemented feature (personal-trainer module). Client-side (mobile Settings) view of active trainers — plain authenticated ROLE_USER.

request.description: Lists this user's active trainers.
Ends the relationship with a trainer. Returns 204.

item.name: Trainer Clients
item.description: Implemented feature (personal-trainer module). Trainer-side (web admin) view of active clients — requires ROLE_TRAINER.

request.description: Lists this trainer's active clients.
Ends the relationship with a client. Returns 204.

item.name: Trainer Client Data
item.description: Implemented feature (personal-trainer module). Read-only view of a specific active client's stats/steps/weight/workouts/meals/avatar for their trainer — requires ROLE_TRAINER and an active trainer-client relationship (403/404 otherwise). Water is deliberately not exposed here (product decision).

query.description: Anchor date; defaults to the server's current date

request.description: Client's stats for today.

query.description: Anchor date; defaults to the server's current date

request.description: Client's stats for the last 7 days.

query.description: Anchor date; defaults to the server's current date

request.description: Client's stats for the last 30 days.
Client's daily step count history, optionally bounded via `from`/`to`.
Client's weight history, optionally bounded via `from`/`to`.
Client's workout session history, paged (newest first), including sets and planned exercises.
Client's logged meals, optionally bounded via `from`/`to`.
Client's daily calorie/protein/carbs/fat goals (from Settings).
Client's profile picture as binary image data. Returns 404 if the client has no picture set.

item.name: Trainer Schedules
item.description: Implemented feature (personal-trainer module). Trainer-scheduled upcoming workout sessions for a client — requires ROLE_TRAINER. Creating a schedule materializes every occurrence immediately as an upcoming workout_sessions row; the client sees them via the normal delta sync.

request.description: Creates a schedule (recurring or one-off) of upcoming sessions for a client. `clientId`, `templateId` (one of the trainer's own workout templates), `recurrence` (ONCE/DAILY/WEEKLY), `startDate` and `endDate` are required (`endDate` is ignored for ONCE but still required on the wire). `daysOfWeek` is required (and only used) for WEEKLY. `timeOfDay` is optional, inherited by every occurrence. Returns 201 and stores the id in `scheduleId`.
Lists a client's active schedules, with done/missed/remaining occurrence counts.
Lists a client's scheduled occurrences in a date range, for the calendar/timeline view: includes upcoming, missed, done and cancelled occurrences. `from`/`to` are required.

Lists every active client's scheduled occurrences in a date range, aggregated across the trainer's whole client roster, for the trainer calendar. `from`/`to` are required and the range cannot span more than 62 days.
Cancels a schedule: soft-deletes its future, not-yet-started occurrences; past (done/missed) occurrences are untouched. Returns 204.
Cancels a single scheduled occurrence. Returns 204, or 409 if it has already started, is in the past, or is already cancelled.

item.name: Trainer Assignments
item.description: Implemented feature (personal-trainer module). Deep-copies a trainer's own workout template/recipe (and everything it references) into a client's account — requires ROLE_TRAINER.

request.description: Assigns a workout template or recipe to a client. `clientId`, `contentType` (TEMPLATE/RECIPE) and `sourceId` (the trainer's own template/recipe id) are required. Re-assigning the same source always creates a fresh copy rather than versioning the previous one — `previouslyAssigned` in the response tells the UI to warn about that. Returns 201 and stores the id in `assignmentId`.
Lists everything this trainer has assigned to a given client.

item.name: List client ids assigned this content

request.description: Lists the client ids this trainer has already assigned this content to. `contentType` and `sourceId` are required. Used by the assign dialog to pre-check clients that already have this template/recipe.
Removes an assignment. Also soft-deletes the client's copy the assignment created. Returns 204.

item.name: Super Admin
item.description: Implemented feature. User list and ROLE_TRAINER grant/revoke — requires ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN. That role is bootstrapped by hand via direct SQL and is never granted/revoked through the API.

request.description: Lists users, paged and optionally searched by email.
Grants a role to a user. Only ROLE_TRAINER can be granted through this endpoint; any other role is 400. Idempotent: granting a role the user already has is a no-op, still 200.
Revokes a role from a user. Returns 204.
Role-change history for a user.
Gets a user's profile picture as binary image data. Returns 404 if no picture is set.
